# Transcrição: 25-How to Assess Stats from Rapid Fire Round #1

- **Duração:** 03:16

## [00:00 - 03:00]

So, as you're running through your rapid fire tests, one of the things that you're going to be looking at is these soft stats. So for example, here we've got our click-through rate and our CPC, and you can also see the beginnings of add to carts happening here. So here, for example, I can kind of look at this one and go, well, I spent $9 and I haven't had any clicks, right? Now it's had almost a thousand impressions. So what I would do, and if I look at this one here, for example, this one's also costing me $4.83 per click. It's got a really low click-through rate, and it's had 2,000 impressions. So I could turn this ad set off. However, it's really important that you're actually really looking at optimizing it properly, because in all reality, it's probably only feeding the budget out to one or two ads. So if we click through on here, you'll see that most of the budget has gone to this ad here because it probably has the most engagement on it. It's a cute baby ad. You'll see here, it's got some social proof starting to build on it, but it's costing us $4.37 for a click, and it has a horrible click-through rate. So really, what I need to do is I need to actually pause this ad so that I can force feed budget into the other ads. Again, we're looking at this one here. It's spent $1.73. No clicks. Well, that's a lot higher than what we want to pay for a click anyway. So I'm going to pause this one as well. And then here, we've got one at $1.50. No clicks. So I'm going to pause this one too. So now I'm left with a couple ads that actually haven't really had much of a chance. This one has had a click at $0.67, a .85 click-through rate. That's okay. We're aiming for 1% here, remember. So it's not too far. It might get better. It hasn't had much reach. So essentially, what we're doing here is we're optimizing this ad set. And this is kind of what you have to do pretty much on a daily basis as you're running through your rapid fire test. Instead of just shutting off the ad sets themselves, go through into the ad level and make sure that Facebook starts feeding budget out into the ads properly. Now once an ad has about a reach of 500, that's when it's going to give you a relevant score. Like you'll see here, there's a relevant score of seven. And when you kind of have that, you sort of know, especially around a thousand impressions, you'll sort of get a feel of what that ad is doing. So if it's costing you a lot of money, like 4.37 here, we have 1,500 impressions. We pretty much know this is going to be consistently the price here. So it was safe to turn this one off here.

## [03:00 - 03:16]

Now this one was not a lot of reach or impression, so I could have left this on as well. However, because I'm working off a really small budget, I really want to get a chance to test other ads out before I roll back to this one. And then potentially I might test these out again down the line.
